description: PUBLISH message with publisher exclusion explicitly disabled

samples.description: PUBLISH with exclude_me=false (publisher receives its own event)

ubjson.note: BJData encoding, a UBJSON superset (autobahn-python >= 26.6.1, bjdata 0.6.x backend)

autobahn-python:
from autobahn.wamp.message import Publish

# Test framework provides 'msg' with deserialized message
assert isinstance(msg, Publish)
assert msg.request == 239714735
assert msg.topic == 'com.myapp.mytopic1'
assert msg.args == ['Hello, world!']
assert msg.kwargs is None or msg.kwargs == {}

# Publisher exclusion explicitly disabled: publisher receives its own event
assert msg.exclude_me is False

construction.autobahn-python:
from autobahn.wamp.message import Publish

msg = Publish(
    request=239714735,
    topic='com.myapp.mytopic1',
    args=['Hello, world!'],
    exclude_me=False
)

notes: When exclude_me is false, the publisher WILL receive an EVENT if it is subscribed to the topic
Default behavior (when exclude_me is not specified) is true - publisher does NOT receive EVENT
This is an Advanced Profile feature that must be supported by the router
The exclude_me option is part of PUBLISH.Options dictionary
